name: Kiros Kai Faki (Klingon Pigs Feet & Pea Soup)
recipeIngredient: Fresh pigs feet or pork hocks, salted
recipeInstructions:
1. The night before making the soup, soak the pigs feet or pork hocks in brine (1 tablespoon of salt per quart of water) until completely covered. Soak the split peas in plain cool water for about 2 inches above the peas; they will swell significantly.
2. In the morning, remove the meat from the brine and place it in an 8-quart pot. Add the peas, onion, and 2 quarts of fresh water. Bring to a boil.
3. Skim any foam that rises to the surface, then reduce heat and simmer for about 6 hours or longer, stirring occasionally to prevent the peas from sticking to the bottom of the pot.
4. Remove the meat and bones from the soup and let cool slightly. Strain the meat off the bones and cut into smaller pieces.
5. Return the meat to the soup if needed and reheat. Serve with a small pat of butter in each bowl.
